retry_reason: Failure only occurs on memory allocation (kzalloc) — retry is pointless if memory is exhausted, and the get path always falls back to create, so no caller retry is needed.
contract_summary: This is a get-or-create wrapper for ucounts structures. It first attempts to find an existing entry and atomically increments its reference count via rcuref_get. If not found, it allocates a new one, initializes its rcuref count to 1, and inserts it into the hash table. In both cases, the caller receives a pointer with a reference they must later release via put_ucounts. On allocation failure, NULL is returned. The function also takes a reference on the associated user namespace via get_user_ns, which is released by put_ucounts.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is get-or-create of ucounts objects: either acquire an existing reference via rcuref_get or initialize a new one with rcuref_init; the remaining logic (lookup, allocation, hash insertion) is minimal setup tightly coupled to refcount lifecycle.

check.reason: rcuref_init(&new->count, 1) sets the rcuref count to 1; the new ucounts pointer is returned. get_user_ns(new->ns) increments the user namespace refcount, which is accessible via parameter ns (the caller's ns argument) with path ns->ns.__ns_ref. Both operations are supported by the code and the access paths and type chains are consistent.
